  "weights": {

  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope do you want for helping with this section? A) Language/style polish only (grammar, clarity, concision). B) Substantive content check: logical consistency, evidence–claim alignment, and novelty/overclaim review without restructuring. C) Section-level reframing/reorganization of the contribution and related positioning (may alter structure/claims) in addition to B. D) Full pass (A+B+C). Please choose A, B, C, or D.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The section makes methodological and causal claims (setup → results → conclusion) that warrant checking for internal logical coherence.",
    "R1.3": "It reports specific F1 gains, costs, and ablation effects that should be verified to support the stated claims as written.",
    "R1.4": "The summary asserts a first-of-its-kind result versus prior detectors, calling for a high-level novelty and delta assessment.",
    "R1.6": "The prose is dense and technical, so sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and concision are likely core to the requested help.",
    "RX.1": "We should surface uncertainties and limits (e.g., benchmark scope, calibration sensitivity) rather than present results as absolute.",
    "RX.2": "Claims should be carefully bounded to avoid overstating SOTA or generality beyond the named benchmarks and detector class.",
    "RX.3": "Assistance will be confined to this section and its contents, avoiding changes to experimental design or adding unsupported material.",
    "RX.4": "Edits should avoid encouraging unsafe operational conclusions by overstating reliability or omitting caveats.",
    "RX.5": "Any claims must remain traceable to the text and citations already present, avoiding invented numbers or unattributed assertions."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope do you want for helping with this section? A) Language/style polish only (grammar, clarity, concision). B) Substantive content check: logical consistency, evidence–claim alignment, and novelty/overclaim review without restructuring. C) Section-level reframing/reorganization of the contribution and related positioning (may alter structure/claims) in addition to B. D) Full pass (A+B+C). Please choose A, B, C, or D.

rationale.R1.2: The section makes methodological and causal claims (setup → results → conclusion) that warrant checking for internal logical coherence.
rationale.R1.3: It reports specific F1 gains, costs, and ablation effects that should be verified to support the stated claims as written.
rationale.R1.4: The summary asserts a first-of-its-kind result versus prior detectors, calling for a high-level novelty and delta assessment.
rationale.R1.6: The prose is dense and technical, so sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and concision are likely core to the requested help.
rationale.RX.1: We should surface uncertainties and limits (e.g., benchmark scope, calibration sensitivity) rather than present results as absolute.
rationale.RX.2: Claims should be carefully bounded to avoid overstating SOTA or generality beyond the named benchmarks and detector class.
rationale.RX.3: Assistance will be confined to this section and its contents, avoiding changes to experimental design or adding unsupported material.
rationale.RX.4: Edits should avoid encouraging unsafe operational conclusions by overstating reliability or omitting caveats.
rationale.RX.5: Any claims must remain traceable to the text and citations already present, avoiding invented numbers or unattributed assertions.
